question: Does the generated image include the curly-haired woman matching the facial features and hair texture of the reference image?
Does the woman's outfit match the reference (white long-sleeve button-up shirt, light blue jeans, and white sneakers)?
Is the black telescope identifiable and consistent with the design and metallic finish of the reference object?
Does the generated image include the pine tree bonsai matching the foliage, curved trunk, and pot style of the reference image?
Is the background the specific rooftop sunset cityscape provided in the reference images?
Is the woman positioned in the foreground on the left side of the frame?
Is the telescope tripod positioned to the front-left of the woman?
Is the bonsai positioned on a side table on the right side of the frame?
Is the entire bonsai visible without being cropped off at the edges?
Are the woman's hands physically touching the telescope (e.g., adjusting focus) with realistic contact (no floating)?
Are the telescope tripod feet firmly planted on the cement ground without floating or sinking?
Is the lighting on the subjects consistent with sunset backlighting, showing golden rim light coming from the right side?
Are there long shadows cast on the ground in a direction consistent with the sunset light source?
Has the woman's original flat studio lighting been successfully converted to high-contrast dusk lighting (shadowed front, lit back/side)?
Does the image exhibit slight sharpening and grain consistent with a smartphone night mode aesthetic?
Are there any visible white or black halos/fringing around the edges of the composited subjects?
Are there any anatomical distortions in the woman's hands or arms?
Are there any severe geometric distortions in the telescope or tripod structure?
